spec_match.rationale: Rewrites the seven deprecated getters to their method forms — directly addresses the migration pain caused by the deprecation.

interactive.rationale: Paste source, click migrate, see rewritten output with per-getter hit counts and a copy-to-clipboard button. Real tool.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises an automated codemod with hit reporting — delivered.

edge_cases.rationale: Regex-based pass will produce false positives on identifiers like `foo.calendars` where `foo` is NOT an Intl.Locale instance (e.g. `myObj.calendars`). The 'legend' line acknowledges this but the demo could at least visually flag low-confidence matches. The default sample includes `loc.currencies` which the legend says has no method form — but the code's `map` doesn't include `currencies` so it's silently skipped. Confusing.

references.rationale: See also links to chromestatus.com/feature/5104985252266240 — WRONG feature ID. This concept belongs to feature 5148228059398144. Critical rule #3 regression.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, deprecated/modern classes use --accent-rose / --accent-emerald colour distinction.

openQuestions.title: Fix the wrong chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: See also points at chromestatus.com/feature/5104985252266240 but this concept belongs to feature 5148228059398144. Slug-recovery would relocate this folder under the wrong feature.

openQuestions.title: Make the `currencies` skip visible
openQuestions.detail: The default sample includes `loc.currencies` and the legend mentions no method form exists. But the codemod silently passes over it. Either drop the line from the sample or visually flag the skipped hit in the output.

openQuestions.title: Add a confidence indicator for ambiguous matches
openQuestions.detail: When the regex matches `someVariable.calendars` where the lhs is unlikely to be an Intl.Locale (e.g. no `Intl.Locale` constructor visible in the file), show a yellow chip indicating low confidence. Production codemods (jscodeshift) need this — the demo could foreshadow it.

summary: Working codemod with real output; ships the WRONG chromestatus feature ID and silently drops the `currencies` line that the sample explicitly includes.
